method_summary: The paper investigates when sparse Mixture of Experts (MoE) models help in vision tasks by examining the role of backbone compute leverage in sparse routing, evaluated under multi-seed protocols on various benchmarks.
key_innovation: Identification of the 'compute-leverage pattern' - discovering that positive accuracy gaps in sparse MoE require a substantial fraction ρ of total FLOPs to be routed, and at ImageNet scale, multi-expert routing (k ≥ 2) is additionally required.

key_results.CIFAR-100: per-sample Soft MoE with softmax over experts rescues performance above dense baseline

title: When Does Sparse MoE Help in Vision? The Role of Backbone Compute Leverage in Sparse Routing
